---
title: "Environment"
description: "CO2 emissions, energy, and environmental indicators — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import os
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar
```

How much carbon Canada emits, how that has changed over time, and how its energy is sourced — shown against OECD peers. Production-based measures count emissions where they physically occur.

## Where Canada Stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("environment").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## CO2 Emissions per Capita

Production-based CO2 emissions per person, in tonnes of CO2. This is the most directly comparable measure of a country's carbon footprint, controlling for population size.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2cap_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_per_capita",
        title="", yaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest CO2 per Capita: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "co2_per_capita", "Tonnes CO2 per capita",
        f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} t CO2/capita<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## CO2 Emissions Trend (Indexed, 2000 = 100)

How each country's CO2 emissions have changed relative to 2000 levels. Values below 100 indicate reductions since 2000; above 100 indicate growth.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2idx_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2idx_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2idx_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_index",
        title="", yaxis_title="Index (2000 = 100)",
    )
    # Add reference line at 100
    fig.add_hline(y=100, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888", opacity=0.5)
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2idx_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 indexed data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Low-Carbon Electricity

The share of electricity generated from non-fossil sources — hydro, nuclear, wind, and solar combined. This is the measure most relevant to decarbonising the grid: it counts nuclear (which "renewables" alone leaves out), and unlike total energy it isn't dominated by the oil and gas still burned for transport and heating.

```{python}
#| echo: false
emix_path = "data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv"
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "low_carbon_share_elec",
        title="", yaxis_title="% of electricity from low-carbon sources",
        show_average=True)
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest Low-Carbon Electricity: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "low_carbon_share_elec", "% of electricity from low-carbon sources",
        f"Source: Our World in Data | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.0f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Electricity Mix by Country

How each country generates electricity, by source (latest available year). Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) versus nuclear and renewables (which include hydro). Countries are sorted cleanest-first.

```{python}
#| echo: false
elec_sources = [
    ("coal_share_elec", "Coal", "#4a4a4a"),
    ("oil_share_elec", "Oil", "#8c564b"),
    ("gas_share_elec", "Natural Gas", "#ff7f0e"),
    ("nuclear_share_elec", "Nuclear", "#9467bd"),
    ("renewables_share_elec", "Renewables (incl. hydro)", "#2ca02c"),
]
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    emix = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    latest_year = emix.dropna(subset=["fossil_share_elec"]).groupby("country_code")["year"].max()
    rows = [emix[(emix["country_code"] == cc) & (emix["year"] == yr)].iloc[0]
            for cc, yr in latest_year.items()
            if not emix[(emix["country_code"] == cc) & (emix["year"] == yr)].empty]
    latest = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("fossil_share_elec", ascending=False)

    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in elec_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
            y=latest["country_name"], x=latest[col].fillna(0),
            name=name, orientation="h", marker_color=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{x:.1f}}%<extra>%{{y}}</extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", xaxis_title="% of electricity generation", yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white", xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0", range=[0, 100]),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.09, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
        height=680, margin=dict(b=180, t=20),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Canada's Electricity Mix Over Time

How Canada generates electricity has shifted over the decades — coal phasing down, with hydro and nuclear supplying most of the grid.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    ce = emix[emix["country_code"] == "CAN"].dropna(subset=["fossil_share_elec"]).sort_values("year")
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in elec_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=ce["year"], y=ce[col].fillna(0), name=name, mode="lines",
            stackgroup="one", line=dict(width=0.5, color=color), fillcolor=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="% of electricity generation",
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
                   rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.08, bgcolor="#f5f5f5"),
                   range=[1999, int(ce["year"].max())]),
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="v", yanchor="top", y=1, xanchor="left", x=1.02),
        margin=dict(b=90, t=20, r=190),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.28,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Electricity mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Total Energy Mix in Canada

Electricity is only part of the picture. This shows Canada's **total primary energy** — including the oil and gas burned for transport and heating, not just electricity — which is why fossil fuels still dominate even though the grid is mostly low-carbon.

```{python}
#| echo: false
energy_sources = [
    ("coal_share_energy", "Coal", "#4a4a4a"),
    ("oil_share_energy", "Oil", "#8c564b"),
    ("gas_share_energy", "Natural Gas", "#ff7f0e"),
    ("nuclear_share_energy", "Nuclear", "#9467bd"),
    ("renewables_share_energy", "Renewables (incl. hydro)", "#2ca02c"),
]
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    canada_emix = emix[emix["country_code"] == "CAN"].dropna(subset=["coal_share_energy"]).sort_values("year")
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in energy_sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=canada_emix["year"], y=canada_emix[col].fillna(0), name=name, mode="lines",
            stackgroup="one", line=dict(width=0.5, color=color), fillcolor=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="% of primary energy supply",
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
                   rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.08, bgcolor="#f5f5f5"),
                   range=[1999, int(canada_emix["year"].max())]),
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="v", yanchor="top", y=1, xanchor="left", x=1.02),
        margin=dict(b=90, t=20, r=190),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.28,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Energy mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Download Data

- [CO2 per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [CO2 Emissions Indexed (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [Energy Mix — electricity & primary (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv) — Our World in Data (Energy Institute, Ember)
